output: US goalkeeper Hope Solo will miss two February matches against France and England after a recent team-related disciplinary issue. The article notes domestic violence charges against Solo were dropped earlier this month and recounts a 2012 positive test for a banned substance, which she says she wasn’t aware contained a banned substance and was cleared of wrongdoing. The U.S. women’s national team is preparing for the World Cup in Canada in June, aiming to win a third title. The report also mentions Solo’s husband, Jerramy Stevens, being arrested for DUI in Los Angeles, with Solo not cited, and Solo later tweeting that she would take a break to decompress and return ready to contribute.
